_reference: FAA Advisory Circular AC 43.13-1B: Acceptable Methods, Techniques, and Practices - Aircraft Inspection and Repair
_units: density = edge_pixels / total_pixels (dimensionless ratio from Canny edge detection)

scratch.description: Linear surface damage caused by abrasive contact

accept.note: Minor surface scratch, no structural compromise

monitor.note: Moderate scratch depth; schedule inspection at next maintenance

reject.note: Deep or extensive scratch exceeding allowable limits

repair_procedures.accept: Polish affected area with 400-grit wet/dry abrasive paper, blend into surrounding surface, apply corrosion inhibitor per MIL-C-16173 Grade 2.
repair_procedures.monitor: Measure scratch depth with profilometer. If depth < 10% of material thickness, blend and re-inspect within 100 flight hours. Apply temporary protective coating.
repair_procedures.reject: Remove component from service. Perform material-removal blending per AC 43.13-1B Fig. 4-28 or replace part. Return to service only after DER sign-off.

pitting.description: Localised corrosion pits on metal surface

monitor.note: Moderate pit density; corrosion may be progressing

repair_procedures.accept: Clean area with Alodine 1200 conversion coating. Apply epoxy primer (MIL-PRF-23377) within 4 hours of treatment.
repair_procedures.monitor: Chemical treatment with phosphoric acid wash. Measure pit depth; if < 5% of wall thickness, treat and re-inspect every 50 flight hours.
repair_procedures.reject: Remove affected section. Inspect adjacent structure for inter-granular corrosion. Replace or blend-repair per AC 43.13-1B Section 6-7(g). Mandatory corrosion inhibitor treatment.

crazing.description: Network of fine surface cracks (stress crazing or solvent crazing in composites/acrylics)
crazing.faa_reference: AC 43.13-1B Chapter 3, Section 3-6 and Chapter 5, Section 5-4

accept.note: Superficial crazing confined to surface layer only

monitor.note: Crazing extends toward substrate; stress concentrations present

repair_procedures.accept: Sand lightly with 320-grit, inspect for depth with dye-penetrant (ASTM E1417). Apply two-part polyurethane topcoat.
repair_procedures.monitor: Dye-penetrant inspection mandatory. If cracks < 0.005 in. deep, sand, apply fibre-reinforced filler, and re-inspect within 25 flight hours.
repair_procedures.reject: Ground aircraft. Perform ultrasonic C-scan per ASTM E2533. Replace panel or perform composite patch repair per AC 43.13-1B Section 3-6. Requires IA sign-off.

inclusion.description: Foreign material (slag, oxide, non-metallic particles) embedded in surface or substrate
inclusion.faa_reference: AC 43.13-1B Chapter 4, Section 4-12 and AMS 2631 ultrasonic inspection

accept.note: Micro-inclusions below AMS allowable size limit

monitor.note: Inclusion cluster detected; further NDE required

reject.note: Inclusion density exceeds AMS 2175 Grade C limits

repair_procedures.accept: Document location and size. Magnetic-particle or fluorescent penetrant inspection every 200 flight hours per AMS 2641.
repair_procedures.monitor: Ultrasonic phased-array scan (AMS 2631). If largest inclusion < 0.050 in. and no cluster, monitor and re-test at 100 flight hours.
repair_procedures.reject: Remove from service immediately. Submit for metallurgical failure analysis per ASTM E2332. Replace with certified material per AMS 2175. Mandatory airworthiness directive review.

repair_procedures.accept: No action required. Continue normal inspection intervals.

unclassified_defect.description: Defect detected but classifier confidence below acceptable threshold; type cannot be reliably determined

accept.note: N/A — unclassified defects are never accepted

monitor.note: N/A — unclassified defects require full evaluation

reject.note: Cannot determine defect severity; reject pending full DER evaluation

repair_procedures.reject: Ground aircraft immediately. Submit component for full NDE evaluation (dye-penetrant, ultrasonic, radiographic as applicable). Obtain DER disposition before return to service. Document in maintenance log per 14 CFR Part 43 Appendix A.
